_pairing: tonPubkey holds each operator's mainnet v4/v5r1 WALLET ADDRESS (the public TON identity we have), not an ed25519 hex pubkey. ALL THREE vizPubkey<->tonPubkey pairings are OPERATOR-CONFIRMED (2026-07-16): op-1 VIZ66354<->EQCk_GXt (raw 0:a4fc65ed…592e, uninitialized/0 TON, offline), op-2 VIZ7broT<->EQAng1Ia (raw 0:2783521a…b5e6, funded ~1.87 TON, this box's live signer), op-3 VIZ81beK<->EQAj-bGk (raw 0:23f9b1a4…303a, ~46 TON active). The prior op-1/op-2 tonPubkey pairing was a mislabel from zipping two independently-ordered lists (vizPubkeys in gram.gate key_auths order; tonPubkeys in multisig signer-array order). NOTE the manifest tonPubkey order NO LONGER matches the on-chain multisig signer index (on-chain: idx0=EQAng1Ia, idx1=EQCk_GXt, idx2=EQAj-bGk); harmless because the approve path derives idx from the signer's actual wallet vs the on-chain set (gramApprove.ts), not from this order. vizPubkey IS READ ON THE RUNTIME PATH — SignerRegistry enforces a signer's recovered VIZ key matches its labeled operator id (mismatch => registration rejected); tonPubkey is NOT registration-checked. Supply real ed25519 tonPubkeys BEFORE relying on this manifest for a TON rotation.
